text.tspan: Decoding LLMs one token at a time is autoregressive: each token waits for the previous.
Speculative decoding: a SMALL draft model proposes K tokens in advance,
the BIG target model verifies them in ONE forward pass.
Result: 2-4x latency improvement at unchanged output quality.
Step 2: BIG model verifies all 4 in ONE forward pass.
If big model agrees with first 3: accept those 3.
If big model disagrees on token 4: replace with big-model's choice.
Result: in best case, 4 tokens per forward pass.
Latency typically halves (or better) at IDENTICAL output.
- Lookahead decoding (no draft model; speculate from history)
- Predicted Outputs (OpenAI Nov 2024 - user provides the prediction)
- Self-speculative (big model drafts cheaply, big model verifies)
Inference platforms: vLLM, TGI, llama.cpp, TensorRT-LLM all support.
API providers: implemented behind-the-scenes (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc).
- Reasoning models (thinking tokens are hard to predict)
